MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Administrator.
Charlie Yu for Hedani, Choy, Spalding and Salvagioni (Objector).

Ms. Rasch stated that they don't have any stake in whatever agreement there is between the Beneficiary and the Law Firm, and that their obligation is to ensure probate is not unnecessarily impeded in distribution. Mr. Yu suggested the Administrator deposit the amount of their claim with the Court and asked for time to perfect their objection. The Court granted the First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. (subject to review of the order). The Court does not see any basis for withholding the amount of the creditor's claim.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Preliminary Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-14-2016 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2450 28th Avenue, San Francisco Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that it has the appraisal and that the sale can proceed. The sale was confirmed at $912,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $530,000.00 is required.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-24-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for and with (petitioner)Debra Dolch. Marta Eisenberg for and with Steven Lehnherr. Michael Spalding for (petitioner) Anita Francisco.

The Court asked what has changed since the last hearing. Mr. Spalding stated that nothing has changed, that Mr. Lehnherr is not otherwise entitled to an appointment and that leave Anita Francisco with standing. Ms. Eisenberg stated that Mr. Lehnherr is the son of the decedent, that he has the ability to nominate an administrator, that he would like Debra Dolch and is confident that she can handle the case smoothly. Ms. Rasch stated her agreement, that Ms. Dolch is licensed with many years of experience, that she would be a neutral party, that the other petitioner is inexperienced, directed attention to a document filed in which Ms. Francisco signed as administrator prior to being appointed and that it is a confidential document in the public file and that both parties come in at the same rate of pay. Mr. Spalding stated that Ms. Francisco has 40 years of experience, that Mr. Lehnherr did not disclose the death of their father to his sister and that he had represented the decedent in the past. Ms. Eisenberg stated that her clients character is not before the court, that Ms. Francisco is an employee of the firm that her counsel is with and that there could be a conflict of interest. The Court took the petition for letters of administration and the petition for letters of administration with limited appointment under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kinnear. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Spalding for (petitioner) Anita Francisco. Steven Lehnherr in Propria Persona. Debra Dolch in Propria Persona.

The Court asked Mr. Lehnherr if he is going to file his own petition. Mr. Lehnherr stated that he has been blindsided, that he objects to the appointment of Anita, that all he knows about Anita is that she works in Mr. Spalding's office, that he wants a neutral party in place and requests that Debra Dolch be appointed. The Court asked Ms. Dolch if she would be obtaining counsel. Ms. Dolch confirmed that she would, stated that she had been called by Mr. Lehnherr with questions and explained the process to him and that she can bring forth a petition. Mr. Spalding stated that the decedent had two children, that there was no contest to Michelle Howard making a nomination and cited P.C. 8465, that he does not think Mr. Lehnherr has entitlement to make a nomination, that Mr. Lehnherr is a non resident heir, that the decedent was a client of Mr. Spalding's years ago, that Anita has 40 years of experience, that she is aware of the obligation, that bond has been set and that he would be available if needed. The Court stated that it wants to give a chance for Mr. Lehnherr to work with Ms. Dolch and get a petition on file if he independently concludes that it makes sense to go forward. Mr. Spalding asked that Mr. Lehnherr be ordered to not dissipate assets pending a further order. The Court suggested that Mr. Lehnherr obtain counsel, view the order and make his submission to the court. The petition for letters of administration was continued to March 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Continued To Jan-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
